name: Easy Apple Custard Pie
recipeIngredient: Uncooked rolled oats (3/4 cup; quick or old-fashioned)
Egg whites (4, slightly beaten) or 2 eggs
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose sweet-tart apples like Granny Smiths, Golden Delicious, Jonathans, or Pippins.
2. Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
3. In a large bowl or electric mixer bowl, mix flour, oats, brown sugar, and salt (if using).
4. Add melted butter, water, and vanilla; mix well.
5. Press the mixture firmly into the bottom and sides of a 9-inch glass pie plate, forming a 1/4-inch rim around the edge.
6. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until lightly golden brown. Cool.
7. In a large bowl, combine sour cream, brown sugar, flour, egg whites (or 2 eggs), cinnamon, and nutmeg. Add apples and toss.
8. Arrange the apple mixture in the crust.
9. For topping, mix rolled oats, brown sugar, and flour in a medium bowl or electric mixer bowl. Add melted butter and mix until it resembles coarse crumbs.
10. Sprinkle the topping over the filling.
11. Bake in the preheated oven at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 50 to 60 minutes or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.
12. Serve warm or chilled; refrigerate leftovers.
